title: SB 1254 (The FRESH Act) connects incarcerated individuals with vital services such as CalFresh and other resources prior to release in order to increase the success of re-entry
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem
raw_preview:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The FRESH Act (SB 1254) aims to reduce recidivism by allowing incarcerated individuals to pre-apply for CalFresh benefits 90 days prior to their release.",

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The FRESH Act (SB 1254) aims to reduce recidivism by allowing incarcerated individuals to pre-apply for CalFresh benefits 90 days prior to their release.",
    "The FRESH Act expands access to CalFresh benefits for incarcerated individuals who would otherwise qualify.",
    "The FRESH Act creates a workgroup within the California Department of Social Services (CDSS) to develop a uniform re-entry process for incarcerated individuals.",
    "Expanding access to benefits for incarcerated individuals increases the likelihood of successful re-entry.",
    "Food insecurity is a significant hurdle for previously-incarcerated individuals upon re-entry.",
    "The FRESH Act will create a process to remove barriers for accessing CalFresh and other supportive services.",
    "The FRESH Act is intended to help reduce food insecurity in California.",
    "The bill requires the CDSS and the Department of Health Care Services to create and test a combined application for Medi-Cal and CalFresh for soon-to-be-released incarcerated individuals by January 1, 2026."
  ]
}
```
